debug_info.goal:
μ ν : Measure ℝ
inst✝¹ : IsProbabilityMeasure μ
inst✝ : IsProbabilityMeasure ν
n : ℕ
i j : Fin n
h : i < j
⊢ HasLaw (fun ω => wignerMatrixMap n ω i j) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n)

failed_attempts: EXCEPTION: TimeoutError:
EXCEPTION: TimeoutError:

have h₁ : (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j) = (fun ω ↦ ω ⟨(i, j), le_of_lt h⟩) := by
    funext ω
    simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
    <;> split_ifs <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_succ]
    <;> (try contradiction)
    <;> (try omega)
    <;> (try aesop)
    <;> (try {
      cases' lt_or_gt_of_ne ‹i ≠ j› with h₂ h₂ <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_succ]
      <;> omega
    })
    <;> (try {
      exfalso
      simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_succ]
      <;> omega
    })
  rw [h₁]
  -- Now we need to show that the projection has the correct law
  -- Since `i < j`, `(i, j)` is not on the diagonal, so we use the measure `μ`
  have h₂ : wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, j), le_of_lt h⟩ = μ := by
    dsimp [wignerMeasureDist]
    simp [h, Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_succ]
    <;> aesop
  -- The projection of the product measure is `μ`
  apply hasLaw_of_injective (fun p q h => by simpa using h)
  <;> simp_all [WignerMeasure, Measure.pi_apply]
  <;> aesop

have h₃ : i < j := h
    have h₄ : ¬(i = j) := by omega
    have h₅ : ¬(j < i) := by omega
    -- We need to show that the law of the random variable is μ
    -- This is done by showing that the random variable is equal to the projection of the Wigner space onto the (i, j)-th entry
    -- and using the fact that the measure on the Wigner space is independent and the (i, j)-th entry has measure μ
    have h₆ : HasLaw (fun ω : (Index n → ℝ) => (ω ⟨(i, j), h⟩)) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      -- Use the fact that the (i, j)-th entry has measure μ
      have h₇ : (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, j), h⟩) = μ := by
        -- Since i < j, the (i, j)-th entry is in the upper triangle and has measure μ
        simp [wignerMeasureDist, h₃.ne]
        <;> aesop
      -- Use the fact that the measure on the Wigner space is independent
      have h₈ : HasLaw (fun ω : (Index n → ℝ) => (ω ⟨(i, j), h⟩)) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, j), h⟩) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
        -- Use the fact that the measure on the Wigner space is independent
        have h₉ : HasLaw (fun ω : (Index n → ℝ) => (ω ⟨(i, j), h⟩)) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, j), h⟩) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
          apply MeasureTheory.pi_hasLaw
          <;> simp [h₇]
        exact h₉
      -- Combine the results
      rw [h₇] at h₈
      exact h₈
    -- Combine the results
    have h₇ : (fun ω : (Index n → ℝ) => (if i < j then ω ⟨(i, j), h⟩ else if j < i then ω ⟨(j, i), by omega⟩ else if i = j then ω ⟨(i, j), by simp_all [le_refl]⟩ else by contradiction)) = (fun ω : (Index n → ℝ) => (ω ⟨(i, j), h⟩)) := by
      funext ω
      simp [h₃]
      <;> aesop
    rw [h₇]
    exact h₆
  exact h₂

have h₁ : (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j) = (fun ω ↦ ω ⟨(i, j), by simpa using h.le⟩) := by
    funext ω
    rw [show (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j = (wignerMatrixMap' ω) i j by rfl]
    rw [show (wignerMatrixMap' ω) i j = (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω) i j by rfl]
    simp only [wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
    split_ifs <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_mk]
    <;>
    (try { contradiction }) <;>
    (try {
      exfalso
      simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_mk]
      <;>
      (try omega)
    }) <;>
    (try {
      simp_all [Index.mk.injEq, Prod.ext_iff]
      <;>
      (try omega)
    }) <;>
    (try {
      cases' lt_or_le i j with h₂ h₂ <;> simp_all [h₂]
      <;>
      (try omega)
    })
    <;>
    (try {
      simp_all [h]
      <;>
      (try omega)
    })
    <;>
    (try {
      simp_all [lt_irrefl]
      <;>
      (try omega)
    })
  
  have h₂ : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    rw [h₁]
    -- Use the fact that the measure on the product space is the product of the measures
    have h₃ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, j), by simpa using h.le⟩) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      -- Each coordinate function in the product space has the corresponding measure
      have h₄ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, j), by simpa using h.le⟩) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, j), by simpa using h.le⟩) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
        apply MeasureTheory.hasLaw_pi
        <;> simp [wignerMeasureDist]
      -- For i < j, the measure is μ
      have h₅ : wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, j), by simpa using h.le⟩ = μ := by
        have h₆ : (⟨(i, j), by simpa using h.le⟩ : Index n).val.1 ≠ (⟨(i, j), by simpa using h.le⟩ : Index n).val.2 := by
          intro h₇
          have h₈ : i = j := by
            simp [Index.val] at h₇ ⊢
            <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
            <;> aesop
          have h₉ : i < j := h
          have h₁₀ : ¬(i < j) := by
            simp_all [h₈]
            <;> omega
          exact h₁₀ h₉
        simp [wignerMeasureDist, h₆]
        <;> aesop
      rw [h₅] at h₄
      exact h₄
    exact h₃
  
  exact h₂

-- 1. Understand the problem
  -- We need to prove that for i < j, the entry (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j has law μ under the WignerMeasure μ ν n.
  -- The Wigner measure is constructed so that:
  -- - Diagonal entries follow ν.
  -- - Off-diagonal entries (i < j) follow μ.
  -- - Symmetric entries (i > j) follow μ using the symmetry of the matrix.
  -- So, (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j directly corresponds to ω ⟨(i, j), _⟩ where ⟨(i, j), _⟩ is in the index set with i < j.
  -- 2. Break down the problem
  -- The Wigner matrix map is defined such that for i < j, (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j = ω ⟨(i, j), _⟩.
  -- The Wigner measure assigns μ to the coordinate ⟨(i, j), _⟩ because i ≠ j.
  -- Therefore, the law of (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j is the same as the law of ω ⟨(i, j), _⟩ which is μ by definition of WignerMeasure.
  -- 3. Formalize the proof
  -- We need to show that the function (fun ω ⇒ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j) has law μ under WignerMeasure μ ν n.
  -- First, we know that (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j = ω ⟨(i, j), _⟩ because i < j.
  -- Then, the coordinate function ω ⟨(i, j), _⟩ has law μ by the definition of WignerMeasure.
  -- A hasLaw statement is just saying that the pushforward of the measure along the function is μ.
  -- Since the pushforward of WignerMeasure along the coordinate function is μ, we can deduce that the pushforward of WignerMeasure along (fun ω ↦ ω ⟨(i, j), _⟩) is μ.
  -- But we need to use the fact that (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j = ω ⟨(i, j), _⟩ to transfer this to (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j).
  -- This is straightforward because the function (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j) is exactly the same as (fun ω ↦ ω ⟨(i, j), _⟩).

  -- 4. Conclusion
  -- The proof can be completed by showing that (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j) is equal to the coordinate function ω ↦ ω ⟨(i, j), _⟩, and then using the properties of the WignerMeasure to assign the law μ.
  have h₁ : (fun ω : WignerSpace n => (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j) = (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, j), by exact le_of_lt h⟩) := by
    funext ω
    simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction, Fin.ext_iff]
    <;>
    (try { contradiction }) <;>
    (try {
      split_ifs at * <;>
      (try { contradiction }) <;>
      (try { simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_mk] }) <;>
      (try { omega })
    }) <;>
    (try {
      aesop
    }) <;>
    (try {
      simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_mk]
      <;>
      omega
    })
  rw [h₁]
  -- Now we need to show that the coordinate function ω ↦ ω ⟨(i, j), _⟩ has law μ under WignerMeasure μ ν n.
  -- This is because ⟨(i, j), _⟩ is an index with i < j, so wignerMeasureDist assigns μ to it.
  -- The WignerMeasure is the product measure of wignerMeasureDist over all indices, so the coordinate function has law μ.
  have h₂ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, j), by exact le_of_lt h⟩) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    have h₃ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, j), by exact le_of_lt h⟩) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, j), by exact le_of_lt h⟩) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      apply MeasureTheory.hasLaw_pi
      <;> simp [wignerMeasureDist]
      <;> aesop
    have h₄ : (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, j), by exact le_of_lt h⟩) = μ := by
      simp [wignerMeasureDist]
      <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
      <;> aesop
    rw [h₄] at h₃
    exact h₃
  exact h₂

have h₁ : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    have h₂ : (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j) = (fun ω => ω ⟨(i, j), h.le⟩) := by
      funext ω
      simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
      <;>
      (try decide) <;>
      (try
        {
          split_ifs <;>
          (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_mk, lt_iff_le_and_ne]) <;>
          (try omega) <;>
          (try
            {
              simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_mk, lt_iff_le_and_ne]
              <;>
              (try omega)
            })
        }) <;>
      (try
        {
          simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_mk, lt_iff_le_and_ne]
          <;>
          (try omega)
        })
      <;>
      (try
        {
          simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_mk, lt_iff_le_and_ne]
          <;>
          (try omega)
        })
    rw [h₂]
    -- Use the fact that the measure is independent to show that each entry has the desired law
    -- and the diagonal entries have the ν measure, while off-diagonal entries have the μ measure
    have h₃ : HasLaw (fun ω => ω ⟨(i, j), h.le⟩) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      -- Use the fact that each coordinate of the product space has the corresponding measure
      -- and the product measure is the product of the measures
      have h₄ : (WignerMeasure μ ν n) = Measure.pi fun (p : Index n) => wignerMeasureDist μ ν n p := by
        rfl
      rw [h₄]
      -- Use the fact that the product measure has the desired property for each coordinate
      have h₅ : wignerMeasureDist μ ν n (⟨(i, j), h.le⟩ : Index n) = μ := by
        -- Check whether the index is diagonal or off-diagonal
        dsimp [wignerMeasureDist]
        -- Since i < j, it is off-diagonal
        have h₆ : (i : ℕ) ≠ (j : ℕ) := by
          intro h₇
          have h₈ : i = j := Fin.ext h₇
          exact lt_irrefl i (by simpa [h₈] using h)
        simp [h₆]
      -- Use the fact that the product measure has the desired property for each coordinate
      have h₆ : HasLaw (fun (ω : Index n → ℝ) => ω ⟨(i, j), h.le⟩)
          (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n (⟨(i, j), h.le⟩ : Index n))
          (Measure.pi fun (p : Index n) => wignerMeasureDist μ ν n p) := by
        apply MeasureTheory.hasLaw_pi_fun
      rw [h₅] at h₆
      exact h₆
    exact h₃
  exact h₁

have h₁ : HasLaw (fun ω => (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    have h₂ : (fun ω => (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j) = (fun ω => ω (⟨(i, j), by
      -- Prove that i ≤ j given i < j
      exact le_of_lt h⟩ : Index n)) := by
      -- Prove that the function is equal to the projection onto the (i, j)-th entry
      funext ω
      rw [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap']
      simp [wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
      split_ifs <;>
        (try { contradiction }) <;>
        (try { simp_all [Fin.ext_iff] <;>
          (try { omega }) <;>
          (try { linarith }) }) <;>
        (try {
          simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
          <;>
          (try { omega }) <;>
          (try { linarith })
          <;>
          (try {
            simp_all [Index.mk.injEq]
            <;>
            (try { omega }) <;>
            (try { linarith })
          })
        }) <;>
        (try {
          simp_all [Index.mk.injEq]
          <;>
          (try { omega }) <;>
          (try { linarith })
          <;>
          (try {
            simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
            <;>
            (try { omega }) <;>
            (try { linarith })
          })
        })
    rw [h₂]
    -- Use the fact that the measure is the product of measures to get the law of the (i, j)-th entry
    have h₃ : HasLaw (fun ω => ω (⟨(i, j), by
      exact le_of_lt h⟩ : Index n)) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      -- Since i < j, the (i, j)-th entry has law μ
      have h₄ : (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n (⟨(i, j), by exact le_of_lt h⟩ : Index n)) = μ := by
        dsimp [wignerMeasureDist]
        by_cases h₅ : (⟨(i, j), by exact le_of_lt h⟩ : Index n).val.1 = (⟨(i, j), by exact le_of_lt h⟩ : Index n).val.2
        <;> simp_all [h₅]
        <;> (try { contradiction })
        <;> (try { linarith })
      -- Use the fact that the measure is the product of measures to get the law of the (i, j)-th entry
      have h₅ : HasLaw (fun ω => ω (⟨(i, j), by exact le_of_lt h⟩ : Index n)) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, j), by exact le_of_lt h⟩) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
        apply MeasureTheory.hasLaw_fst
        <;> simp_all [WignerMeasure, Measure.pi]
      -- Since the (i, j)-th entry has law μ, we get the desired result
      have h₆ : HasLaw (fun ω => ω (⟨(i, j), by exact le_of_lt h⟩ : Index n)) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
        convert h₅ using 1
        <;> simp_all [h₄]
      exact h₆
    exact h₃
  exact h₁

have h₁ : (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j) = (fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ ω ⟨(i, j), h.le⟩ : WignerSpace n → ℝ) := by
    -- Prove that (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j = ω ⟨(i, j), h.le⟩
    funext ω
    simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction, h, lt_iff_le_and_ne]
    <;>
    (try decide) <;>
    (try {
      split_ifs <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
      <;> try { exfalso; linarith } <;> try { omega }
    }) <;>
    (try {
      cases' le_total i j with h₁ h₁ <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
      <;> try { exfalso; omega }
    }) <;>
    (try {
      aesop
    })
  rw [h₁]
  -- Use the fact that the projection map is measurable and the product measure to show the HasLaw property
  have h₂ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ ω ⟨(i, j), h.le⟩) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    have h₃ : (WignerMeasure μ ν n) = Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n) := rfl
    rw [h₃]
    -- Use the product measure to get the HasLaw property for the projection
    have h₄ : (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, j), h.le⟩ : Measure ℝ) = μ := by
      simp [wignerMeasureDist, h, Fin.ext_iff, lt_iff_le_and_ne]
      <;>
      (try decide) <;>
      (try {
        simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
        <;> aesop
      })
    -- Use the fact that the projection is measurable and the product measure to get the HasLaw property
    have h₅ : HasLaw (fun ω : Index n → ℝ => ω ⟨(i, j), h.le⟩) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, j), h.le⟩) (Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n)) := by
      apply MeasureTheory.hasLaw_fst
    convert h₅ using 1
    <;> simp_all
    <;>
    (try {
      aesop
    })
  exact h₂

have h₁ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    have h₂ : (fun ω : WignerSpace n => (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j) = (fun ω : WignerSpace n => (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j)) := rfl
    rw [h₂]
    have h₃ : (i : ℕ) < (j : ℕ) := by
      exact_mod_cast h
    have h₄ : ((i : ℕ)) < ((j : ℕ)) := by
      exact_mod_cast h
    have h₅ : (i : Fin n) < (j : Fin n) := h
    have h₆ : (fun ω : WignerSpace n => (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j)) = (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, j), by
      -- Prove that i ≤ j
      have h₇ : (i : ℕ) < (j : ℕ) := by exact_mod_cast h
      have h₈ : (i : ℕ) ≤ (j : ℕ) := by linarith
      exact Fin.ext_iff.1 (by simpa [h₇] using h₈)
      ⟩) := by
      funext ω
      -- Simplify the expression for the case i < j
      simp [wignerMatrixEntryFunction, h₄, h₅, Fin.ext_iff]
      <;>
      (try omega) <;>
      (try aesop) <;>
      (try norm_num) <;>
      (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]) <;>
      (try omega)
    rw [h₆]
    -- Use the fact that the coordinate projections are independent and have the correct distribution
    have h₇ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, j), by
      have h₈ : (i : ℕ) < (j : ℕ) := by exact_mod_cast h
      have h₉ : (i : ℕ) ≤ (j : ℕ) := by linarith
      exact Fin.ext_iff.1 (by simpa [h₈] using h₉)
      ⟩) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      -- Use the fact that the coordinate projections are independent and have the correct distribution
      have h₈ : (⟨(i, j), by
        have h₉ : (i : ℕ) < (j : ℕ) := by exact_mod_cast h
        have h₁₀ : (i : ℕ) ≤ (j : ℕ) := by linarith
        exact Fin.ext_iff.1 (by simpa [h₉] using h₁₀)
        ⟩ : Index n) ≠ ⟨(i, i), by simp [le_rfl]⟩ := by
        intro h₉
        simp [Index.ext_iff, Fin.ext_iff, h] at h₉
        <;> simp_all [h₃]
        <;> omega
      have h₉ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, j), by
        have h₁₀ : (i : ℕ) < (j : ℕ) := by exact_mod_cast h
        have h₁₁ : (i : ℕ) ≤ (j : ℕ) := by linarith
        exact Fin.ext_iff.1 (by simpa [h₁₀] using h₁₁)
        ⟩) (if (⟨(i, j), by
        have h₁₀ : (i : ℕ) < (j : ℕ) := by exact_mod_cast h
        have h₁₁ : (i : ℕ) ≤ (j : ℕ) := by linarith
        exact Fin.ext_iff.1 (by simpa [h₁₀] using h₁₁)
        ⟩ : Index n).val.1 = (⟨(i, j), by
        have h₁₀ : (i : ℕ) < (j : ℕ) := by exact_mod_cast h
        have h₁₁ : (i : ℕ) ≤ (j : ℕ) := by linarith
        exact Fin.ext_iff.1 (by simpa [h₁₀] using h₁₁)
        ⟩ : Index n).val.2 then ν else μ) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
        apply MeasureTheory.hasLaw_coe
        <;> simp [WignerMeasure]
        <;> aesop
      -- Simplify the condition in the `if` statement
      have h₁₀ : ¬((⟨(i, j), by
        have h₁₁ : (i : ℕ) < (j : ℕ) := by exact_mod_cast h
        have h₁₂ : (i : ℕ) ≤ (j : ℕ) := by linarith
        exact Fin.ext_iff.1 (by simpa [h₁₁] using h₁₂)
        ⟩ : Index n).val.1 = (⟨(i, j), by
        have h₁₁ : (i : ℕ) < (j : ℕ) := by exact_mod_cast h
        have h₁₂ : (i : ℕ) ≤ (j : ℕ) := by linarith
        exact Fin.ext_iff.1 (by simpa [h₁₁] using h₁₂)
        ⟩ : Index n).val.2) := by
        simp_all [Index.ext_iff, Fin.ext_iff]
        <;> omega
      -- Simplify the `if` statement using the fact that the condition is false
      have h₁₁ : (if (⟨(i, j), by
        have h₁₂ : (i : ℕ) < (j : ℕ) := by exact_mod_cast h
        have h₁₃ : (i : ℕ) ≤ (j : ℕ) := by linarith
        exact Fin.ext_iff.1 (by simpa [h₁₂] using h₁₃)
        ⟩ : Index n).val.1 = (⟨(i, j), by
        have h₁₂ : (i : ℕ) < (j : ℕ) := by exact_mod_cast h
        have h₁₃ : (i : ℕ) ≤ (j : ℕ) := by linarith
        exact Fin.ext_iff.1 (by simpa [h₁₂] using h₁₃)
        ⟩ : Index n).val.2 then ν else μ) = μ := by
        by_cases h₁₂ : (⟨(i, j), by
          have h₁₃ : (i : ℕ) < (j : ℕ) := by exact_mod_cast h
          have h₁₄ : (i : ℕ) ≤ (j : ℕ) := by linarith
          exact Fin.ext_iff.1 (by simpa [h₁₃] using h₁₄)
          ⟩ : Index n).val.1 = (⟨(i, j), by
          have h₁₃ : (i : ℕ) < (j : ℕ) := by exact_mod_cast h
          have h₁₄ : (i : ℕ) ≤ (j : ℕ) := by linarith
          exact Fin.ext_iff.1 (by simpa [h₁₃] using h₁₄)
          ⟩ : Index n).val.2
        · exfalso
          exact h₁₀ h₁₂
        · simp [h₁₂]
      -- Combine the results to get the final statement
      rw [h₁₁] at h₉
      exact h₉
    exact h₇
  exact h₁

have h₁ : (fun ω : WignerSpace n => (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j) = (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, j), h⟩) := by
    funext ω
    simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction, h]
    <;>
    (try cases n <;> simp_all (config := {decide := true})) <;>
    (try { simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_mk, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one, Fin.val_succ] <;>
      norm_num <;>
      omega }) <;>
    (try { simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_mk, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one, Fin.val_succ] <;>
      norm_num <;>
      omega }) <;>
    (try {
      simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_mk, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one, Fin.val_succ]
      <;>
      aesop
    })
    <;>
    (try {
      simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_mk, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one, Fin.val_succ]
      <;>
      norm_num at *
      <;>
      omega
    })
    <;>
    simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_mk, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one, Fin.val_succ]
    <;>
    aesop
  
  have h₂ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, j), h⟩) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    have h₃ : (WignerMeasure μ ν n) = Measure.pi (fun (p : Index n) => if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then ν else μ) := rfl
    have h₄ : (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, j), h⟩) = (fun x : WignerSpace n => x (⟨(i, j), h⟩ : Index n)) := by simp
    rw [h₄]
    -- Use the property of the product measure to get the law of the projection
    have h₅ : (⟨(i, j), h⟩ : Index n).val.1 ≠ (⟨(i, j), h⟩ : Index n).val.2 := by
      dsimp [Index] at *
      intro h₆
      simp_all [Prod.ext_iff]
      <;>
      (try contradiction) <;>
      (try omega)
    -- Use the fact that the measure on the product space is the product of the measures
    have h₆ : (if (⟨(i, j), h⟩ : Index n).val.1 = (⟨(i, j), h⟩ : Index n).val.2 then ν else μ) = μ := by
      split_ifs <;> simp_all
      <;>
      (try contradiction) <;>
      (try omega)
    -- Apply the lemma about the law of the projection
    have h₇ : HasLaw (fun x : WignerSpace n => x (⟨(i, j), h⟩ : Index n)) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      have h₈ : (WignerMeasure μ ν n) = Measure.pi (fun (p : Index n) => if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then ν else μ) := rfl
      rw [h₈]
      apply MeasureTheory.hasLaw_pi_of_injective
      <;> simp_all [Function.Injective]
      <;>
      (try {
        intros x y h₉
        simp_all [Index.ext_iff]
        <;>
        aesop
      })
      <;>
      (try {
        use fun i => if i = (⟨(i, j), h⟩ : Index n) then (0 : ℝ) else 0
        <;>
        simp_all [Index.ext_iff]
        <;>
        aesop
      })
      <;>
      aesop
    exact h₇
  
  have h₃ : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    rw [h₁] at *
    exact h₂
  
  exact h₃

have h₁ : (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j) = (fun ω ↦ wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j) := by

    simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', Matrix.of_apply]
    <;>

          <;>
          (try aesop) <;>
          (try contradiction)
        }) <;>
      (try
        {
          simp_all [wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
          <;>
          (try aesop) <;>
          (try contradiction)
        }) <;>
      (try
        {
          simp_all [wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
          <;>
          (try aesop) <;>
          (try contradiction)
        })
      <;>
      (try simp_all [h])
      <;>
      (try
        {
          cases i <;> cases j <;> simp_all (config := {decide := true})
          <;>
          (try omega)
          <;>
          (try contradiction)
        })
      <;>
      (try
        {
          simp_all [h]
          <;>
          (try aesop)
        })
      <;>
      (try
        {
          aesop
        })
    rw [h₃]
    have h₄ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => (ω (⟨(i, j), le_of_lt h⟩) : ℝ)) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      have h₅ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => (ω (⟨(i, j), le_of_lt h⟩) : ℝ)) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, j), le_of_lt h⟩) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
        apply coordinates_measurableSpace'.hasLaw
        <;> simp
      have h₆ : wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, j), le_of_lt h⟩ = μ := by
        simp [wignerMeasureDist]
        <;>
        (try
          {
            simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
            <;>
            (try omega)
            <;>
            (try aesop)
          })
        <;>
        (try
          {
            aesop
          })
        <;>
        (try
          {
            simp_all [wignerMeasureDist]
            <;>
            (try aesop)
          })
        <;>
        (try
          {
            simp_all [h]
            <;>
            (try aesop)
          })
      rw [h₆] at h₅
      exact h₅
    exact h₄
  exact h₂

  have h₁ : (fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ Matrix.of (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω) i j) = fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ ω (⟨(i, j), h⟩ : Index n) := by

    }) <;>
    (try {

    })
  rw [h₁]

    }) <;>
    (try {

      apply MeasureTheory.hasLaw_pi

have h₁ : (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j) = (fun ω ↦ ω ⟨(i, j), by
    exact le_of_lt h⟩) := by
    funext ω
    simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
    <;>
    by_cases h₂ : i = j <;>
    by_cases h₃ : i < j <;>
    simp_all [lt_iff_le_and_ne, Fin.ext_iff]
    <;>
    (try { contradiction }) <;>
    (try { aesop }) <;>
    (try {
      cases' lt_or_gt_of_ne h₂ with h₄ h₄ <;>
      simp_all [lt_iff_le_and_ne, Fin.ext_iff] <;>
      omega })
    <;>
    (try { exfalso; omega })
  
  rw [h₁]
  have h₂ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ ω ⟨(i, j), by exact le_of_lt h⟩) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    -- Use the fact that the measure on the product space assigns μ to the coordinate ⟨(i, j), _⟩
    have h₃ : wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, j), by exact le_of_lt h⟩ = μ := by
      dsimp [wignerMeasureDist]
      -- Since i < j, i ≠ j
      have h₄ : (⟨(i, j), by exact le_of_lt h⟩ : Index n).val.1 ≠ (⟨(i, j), by exact le_of_lt h⟩ : Index n).val.2 := by
        simp [Fin.ext_iff]
        <;>
        omega
      simp [h₄]
      <;>
      aesop
    -- The projection map is measurable and the law is μ
    have h₄ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ ω ⟨(i, j), by exact le_of_lt h⟩) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, j), by exact le_of_lt h⟩) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      apply MeasureTheory.hasLaw_pi
    rw [h₃] at h₄
    exact h₄
  exact h₂

have h₁ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    -- Define the projection that maps each WignerSpace n to the (i,j) entry of the matrix
    have h₂ : (fun ω : WignerSpace n => (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j) = (fun p : Index n => (if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then (1 : ℝ) else 0 : ℝ)) ∘ˢ (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, j), h.le⟩) := by
      ext ω
      -- Simplify the expression to show that the projection is indeed mapping to the correct entry
      simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction, h, le_of_lt, Matrix.of_apply]
      <;>
      (try cases i <;> cases j <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_succ]) <;>
      (try omega) <;>
      (try aesop)
      <;>
      (try
        {
          by_cases h₁ : i = j <;>
          by_cases h₂ : i < j <;>
          by_cases h₃ : j < i <;>
          simp_all [h₁, h₂, h₃, le_of_lt] <;>
          (try omega) <;>
          (try aesop)
        })
      <;>
      (try simp_all [h, le_of_lt])
      <;>
      (try omega)
      <;>
      (try aesop)
      <;>
      (try
        {
          simp_all [h, le_of_lt]
          <;>
          aesop
        })
    rw [h₂]
    -- Use the fact that the projections are independent and have the correct distribution
    have h₃ : HasLaw (fun p : Index n => (if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then (1 : ℝ) else 0 : ℝ)) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      have h₄ : (fun p : Index n => (if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then (1 : ℝ) else 0 : ℝ)) = (fun p : Index n => (if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then (1 : ℝ) else 0 : ℝ)) := rfl
      rw [h₄]
      -- The projection has law μ because it is independent of the other entries
      have h₅ : (WignerMeasure μ ν n) = Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n) := rfl
      rw [h₅]
      -- Use the fact that the measure is the product of measures on each coordinate
      have h₆ : HasLaw (fun p : Index n => (if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then (1 : ℝ) else 0 : ℝ)) μ (Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n)) := by
        -- The measure on the (i,j) entry is μ because i ≠ j
        have h₇ : i ≠ j := by
          intro h₈
          have h₉ : i = j := h₈
          have h₁₀ : ¬(i < j) := by
            intro h₁₁
            have h₁₂ : i < j := h₁₁
            have h₁₃ : i < i := by
              simpa [h₉] using h₁₂
            exact lt_irrefl i h₁₃
          exact h₁₀ h
        -- The measure on the (i,j) entry is μ because i ≠ j
        have h₈ : (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n) ⟨(i, j), h.le⟩ = μ := by
          simp [wignerMeasureDist, h₇]
          <;>
          (try aesop)
          <;>
          (try omega)
        -- Use the fact that the measure is the product of measures on each coordinate
        have h₉ : HasLaw (fun p : Index n => (if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then (1 : ℝ) else 0 : ℝ)) μ (Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n)) := by
          -- The measure on the (i,j) entry is μ because i ≠ j
          have h₁₀ : HasLaw (fun p : Index n => (if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then (1 : ℝ) else 0 : ℝ)) μ (Measure.pi (fun _ : Index n => μ)) := by
            -- The measure on the (i,j) entry is μ because i ≠ j
            simpa using
              ((Pi.hasLaw_const _ _ μ)).mono_right (by intro; simp)
          -- Use the fact that the measure is the product of measures on each coordinate
          have h₁₁ : (Measure.pi (fun _ : Index n => μ)) = Measure.pi (fun _ : Index n => μ) := rfl
          have h₁₂ : (Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n)) = Measure.pi (fun p : Index n => if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then ν else μ) := rfl
          -- Use the fact that the measure is the product of measures on each coordinate
          have h₁₃ : HasLaw (fun p : Index n => (if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then (1 : ℝ) else 0 : ℝ)) μ (Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n)) := by
            -- The measure on the (i,j) entry is μ because i ≠ j
            convert h₁₀ using 1
            <;>
            (try ext; simp_all [wignerMeasureDist, h₇])
            <;>
            (try aesop)
            <;>
            (try omega)
          exact h₁₃
        exact h₉
      exact h₆
    -- Use the fact that the projections are independent and have the correct distribution
    have h₅ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, j), h.le⟩) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, j), h.le⟩) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) :=
      by
      -- The measure on the (i,j) entry is μ because i ≠ j
      have h₆ : (WignerMeasure μ ν n) = Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n) := rfl
      rw [h₆]
      -- Use the fact that the measure is the product of measures on each coordinate
      have h₇ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, j), h.le⟩) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, j), h.le⟩) (Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n)) := by
        -- The measure on the (i,j) entry is μ because i ≠ j
        apply Pi.hasLaw_pi
      exact h₇
    -- Use the fact that the projections are independent and have the correct distribution
    have h₆ : i ≠ j := by
      intro h₇
      have h₈ : i = j := h₇
      have h₉ : ¬(i < j) := by
        intro h₁₀
        have h₁₁ : i < j := h₁₀
        have h₁₂ : i < i := by
          simpa [h₈] using h₁₁
        exact lt_irrefl i h₁₂
      exact h₉ h
    -- Use the fact that the projections are independent and have the correct distribution
    have h₇ : (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, j), h.le⟩) = μ := by
      simp [wignerMeasureDist, h₆]
      <;>
      (try aesop)
      <;>
      (try omega)
    -- Use the fact that the projections are independent and have the correct distribution
    have h₈ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, j), h.le⟩) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      convert h₅ using 1
      <;>
      simp_all [h₇]
      <;>
      (try aesop)
      <;>
      (try omega)
    -- Use the fact that the projections are independent and have the correct distribution
    have h₉ : HasLaw (fun p : Index n => (if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then (1 : ℝ) else 0 : ℝ)) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      convert h₃ using 1 <;>
      simp_all [WignerMeasure]
      <;>
      (try aesop)
      <;>
      (try omega)
    -- Use the fact that the projections are independent and have the correct distribution
    have h₁₀ : HasLaw ((fun p : Index n => (if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then (1 : ℝ) else 0 : ℝ)) ∘ˢ (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, j), h.le⟩)) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      -- Use the fact that the projections are independent and have the correct distribution
      have h₁₁ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, j), h.le⟩) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := h₈
      -- Use the fact that the projections are independent and have the correct distribution
      have h₁₂ : HasLaw ((fun p : Index n => (if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then (1 : ℝ) else 0 : ℝ)) ∘ˢ (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, j), h.le⟩)) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n) :=
        HasLaw.comp h₃ h₁₁
      exact h₁₂
    exact h₁₀
  exact h₁

have h₁ : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    have h₂ : (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j) = (fun ω ↦ ω ⟨(i, j), by exact le_of_lt h⟩) := by

                  simp_all [lt_irrefl]

                })
    rw [h₂]
    have h₃ : HasLaw (fun ω : Index n → ℝ => ω ⟨(i, j), by exact le_of_lt h⟩) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      have h₄ : (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, j), by exact le_of_lt h⟩) = μ := by
        dsimp [wignerMeasureDist]
        have h₅ : (⟨(i, j), by exact le_of_lt h⟩ : Index n).val.1 ≠ (⟨(i, j), by exact le_of_lt h⟩ : Index n).val.2 := by
          simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
          <;>
            (try omega)
          <;>
            (try aesop)
        simp_all [h₅]
      have h₅ : HasLaw (fun ω : Index n → ℝ => ω ⟨(i, j), by exact le_of_lt h⟩) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, j), by exact le_of_lt h⟩) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
        apply MeasureTheory.Basic.hasLaw_pi
      rw [h₄] at h₅
      exact h₅
    exact h₃
  exact h₁

have h₁ : HasLaw (fun ω : Index n → ℝ => (fun ω : Index n → ℝ => ω ⟨(i, j), by
    exact le_of_lt h⟩)) (μ : Measure ℝ) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    rw [show (fun ω : (Index n → ℝ) => (fun ω : (Index n → ℝ) => ω ⟨(i, j), by
      exact le_of_lt h⟩)) = (fun ω : (Index n → ℝ) => ω ⟨(i, j), by
      exact le_of_lt h⟩) by rfl]
    -- Use the definition of WignerMeasure to get the measure on each coordinate
    have h₂ : (WignerMeasure μ ν n) = Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n) := by
      rfl
    rw [h₂]
    -- Use the fact that the coordinates are independent
    have h₃ : (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n) ⟨(i, j), by
      exact le_of_lt h⟩ = μ := by
      simp [wignerMeasureDist]
      <;>
      (try decide <;>
        simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_mk, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one]
        <;>
        norm_num <;>
        omega) <;>
      aesop
    rw [Measure.pi_apply]
    <;> simp_all [h₃]
    <;> aesop
  -- Relate the coordinate to the matrix entry
  have h₂ : (fun ω : Index n → ℝ => (fun ω : Index n → ℝ => ω ⟨(i, j), by
    exact le_of_lt h⟩)) = (fun ω : Index n → ℝ => (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j)) := by
    funext ω
    simp [wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
    <;>
    (try aesop <;>
      simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_mk, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one]
      <;>
      norm_num <;>
      omega) <;>
    aesop
  rw [h₂] at h₁
  -- Relate the matrix entry to the wignerMatrixMap
  have h₃ : (fun ω : Index n → ℝ => (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j)) = (fun ω : Index n → ℝ =>
    (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j) := by
    funext ω
    simp [wignerMatrixMap, Matrix.of_apply, wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
    <;>
    (try aesop <;>
      simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_mk, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one]
      <;>
      norm_num <;>
      omega) <;>
    aesop
  rw [h₃] at h₁
  exact h₁
